project.name: Arduino Teensy Musical Stepper Motor MIDI Player
project.purpose: Converts MIDI file notes to stepper motor frequencies, using SD card storage for MIDI files

control_pin.name: STEP_PIN
control_pin.function: Toggle frequency for stepper motor step pulses
control_pin.logic: digitalWrite toggles between HIGH/LOW at calculated frequency

timing_hardware.purpose: Generate precise frequency pulses for stepper motor control

timing_approach.mechanism: Toggles STEP_PIN state at interrupt intervals
timing_approach.factor_of_2: Each toggle is half period, so *2 in denominator

frequency_generation.conversion: Musical note to Hz to motor-safe frequency

running_status.purpose: MIDI format efficiency - avoids redundant status bytes

variable_length.algorithm: Accumulate 7-bit chunks, continue while MSB set

state_management.lastStatus: Tracks previous MIDI status byte for running status
state_management.state: volatile bool for ISR STEP_PIN state
state_management.timer: IntervalTimer object, global scope for control

frequency_bounds.reason: Stepper motor safe operating range - avoids stalling and excessive speed

hardware_timing_constraints.isr_frequency: Depends on MIDI note; toggles STEP_PIN at 30-400 times per second

header.division: 2 bytes (ticks per quarter note, big-endian uint16)

subtypes.other_meta: Read and skip (length determined by readVar())

running_status.description: If status byte < 0x80, reuse last status
running_status.implementation: seek() back one byte when detected

unsupported: Polyphony (only plays one note at a time)
Tempo changes are parsed but not applied

key_outputs: Startup confirmations (START, SD OK, FILE OK)

gpio.volatile_operations: stepISR() executes asynchronously from main loop

header_parsing: 2. Skip header length and format/track fields
3. Extract division (ticks per quarter note) - typically 480
track_location: 1. Scan through file looking for 'MTrk' identifier
2. Read track length (variable, could span multiple tracks)
3. Break after finding first track (ignores subsequent tracks)
event_loop: 1. Read delta time (readVar()) - ticks since last event
2. Convert delta to milliseconds using division and hardcoded 105 BPM
3. Read status byte (or use running status)
4. Execute event (note on/off, meta event, etc.)
5. For NOTE ON: convert to frequency, start motor, delay, stop motor
motor_control: 1. noteToFreq(note) converts MIDI note to safe stepper frequency
2. play(freq) starts IntervalTimer with calculated period
3. ISR toggles STEP_PIN at the frequency
4. play(0) stops the motor after note duration

note_scaling_factor.purpose: Converts MIDI note frequencies to stepper-safe speeds
note_scaling_factor.effect: A4 (440 Hz) becomes 52.8 Hz after scaling

frequency_bounds.description: Hardware safety limits for stepper motor operation

bpm_hardcoded.note: Tempo meta-events are parsed but not applied (hardcoded BPM overrides)

tempo_default.usage: Stored in tempo variable but not actually used in timing

division_default.standard: Common MIDI standard, overridden by file header

minimum_note_duration.purpose: Prevents timer from being set with too-small periods

code_flow_example.scenario: Playing Mario theme quarter note A4 (note 69)

known_limitations: Tempo changes in MIDI are parsed but ignored; 105 BPM is hardcoded
Only processes first track in multi-track MIDI files
No polyphonic support (would require multiple timers)
Velocity information is read but not used (no dynamic speed control)
Assumes A4=69 standard MIDI note numbering
debug_output_examples: SD OK - SD card initialized successfully
NOTE ON: 69 vel=100 - MIDI event interpreted
Motor ON, freq=52 - Stepper motor started

development_notes.note_to_freq_formula: MIDI standard: f(n) = 440 * 2^((n-69)/12), where n=69 is A4
development_notes.stepper_scaling: 0.12 factor empirically determined for this stepper motor to avoid stalling
development_notes.running_status_safety: seek() called when detected, which handles file position correctly
development_notes.isr_volatile_state: volatile keyword necessary for ISR access to state variable
development_notes.frequency_period: Microsecond period divided by 2 because ISR toggles (both edges count)
development_notes.timing_precision: IntervalTimer provides microsecond precision for accurate frequencies
